title: forestiera reticulata
intro: Forestiera reticulata, also known as Mexican elder or Texas elder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The health benefits of Forestiera reticulata include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, aid in weight loss, and reduce the risk of certain diseases such as diabetes and cardiovascular disease. Forestiera reticulata can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its bark and leaves can also be used to make infusions and decoctions for medicinal purposes.
